Episteme 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
  1. org.episteme.social.economics

Uses of Package
org.episteme.social.economics

Packages that use org.episteme.social.economics
Package
Description
org.episteme.social.architecture
Provides mathematical and analytical models for architectural engineering, building physics, and urban history.
org.episteme.social.architecture.traffic
Provides models and simulation engines for urban traffic flow analysis.
org.episteme.social.arts
- artworks, music, literature classification.
org.episteme.social.arts.music
- musical notes, instruments, compositions.
org.episteme.social.arts.theater
- plays, performances, dramatic arts.
org.episteme.social.economics
Provides comprehensive mathematical models for economic systems, agent-based modeling, and financial analysis.
org.episteme.social.economics.loaders
 
org.episteme.social.economics.models
 
org.episteme.social.economics.money
Provides classes for monetary and financial operations.
org.episteme.social.economics.resources
Provides classes and interfaces for representing economic resources.
org.episteme.social.geography
Provides classes for social geography, human settlements, and mapping.
org.episteme.social.history.archeology
Provides classes and interfaces for archaeological research and analysis.
org.episteme.social.law
Provides the org.episteme.social.law module.
org.episteme.social.medicine.consultation
Core functionality for the org.episteme.social.medicine.consultation package in the Episteme library.
org.episteme.social.politics
Provides the org.episteme.social.politics module.
org.episteme.social.sociology
- social structures, persons, and cultural modeling.
org.episteme.social.ui.viewers.economics
Social sciences modeling, including economics, political science, and sociology.
  • Classes in org.episteme.social.economics used by org.episteme.social.architecture
    Class
    Description
    MaterialResource
    Represents a physical or tangible resource with a unique identification (serial number).
    PotentialResource
    A class representing a potential or virtual resource, such as oil in the soil that is expected to be found, or ingredients required in a recipe or task.
    Property
    An interface defining ownership and value of assets.
    Resource
    A class representing a physical or virtual resource with ownership, position, and temporal attributes.
  • Classes in org.episteme.social.economics used by org.episteme.social.architecture.traffic
    Class
    Description
    MaterialResource
    Represents a physical or tangible resource with a unique identification (serial number).
    PotentialResource
    A class representing a potential or virtual resource, such as oil in the soil that is expected to be found, or ingredients required in a recipe or task.
    Property
    An interface defining ownership and value of assets.
    Resource
    A class representing a physical or virtual resource with ownership, position, and temporal attributes.
  • Classes in org.episteme.social.economics used by org.episteme.social.arts
    Class
    Description
    Community
    Represents a primitive or cooperative social group where resources are shared.
    EconomicAgent
    Represents an autonomous actor in an economic system, which can be an individual, a household, or a firm.
    MaterialResource
    Represents a physical or tangible resource with a unique identification (serial number).
    Organization
    Represents a formal social and economic entity (Company, NGO, Government).
    PotentialResource
    A class representing a potential or virtual resource, such as oil in the soil that is expected to be found, or ingredients required in a recipe or task.
    Property
    An interface defining ownership and value of assets.
    Resource
    A class representing a physical or virtual resource with ownership, position, and temporal attributes.
    Worker
    Represents a worker within an Organization.

    Class
    Description

    A class representing a potential or virtual resource, such as oil in the soil that is expected to be found, or ingredients required in a recipe or task.

  • Classes in org.episteme.social.economics used by org.episteme.social.economics
    Class
    Description
    AuctionSimulator.AuctionResult
    Result summary for a completed auction simulation.
    AuctionSimulator.Bid
    Individual bid entry in an auction history.
    AuctionSimulator.Bidder
    Model for an individual auction participant.
    AuctionTheorySolver.Bid
    Represents a bid in an auction.
    Bank
    Represents a banking institution capable of managing accounts and providing currency conversion services.
    Community
    Represents a primitive or cooperative social group where resources are shared.
    Department
    Extensible enumeration for Organizational Department.
    EconomicAgent
    Represents an autonomous actor in an economic system, which can be an individual, a household, or a firm.
    EconomicProcess
    Extensible enumeration for Economic Process.
    EconomicSituation
    Represents a social situation involving the interaction of people around economic resources and activities.
    Economy
    Functional abstraction for an economic system, encompassing organizations, central banking, and macro-financial indicators.
    GameTheoryEngine.NashEquilibrium
    Details of a strategic equilibrium point.
    GameTheoryEngine.NormalFormGame
    Data model for a 2-player game in normal form.
    GameTheorySolver.Payoff
    Data structure for 2-player payoff entries.
    IndustryCategory
    Extensible enumeration for Industry Category.
    IndustrySector
    Extensible enumeration for Industry Sector.
    ManagementLevel
    Extensible enumeration for Management Level.
    MarketStructure
    Extensible enumeration for Market Structure.
    MaterialResource
    Represents a physical or tangible resource with a unique identification (serial number).
    OptionPricer.OptionGreeks
     
    OptionPricer.OptionParameters
     
    OptionPricer.OptionStyle
     
    OptionPricer.OptionType
     
    OptionPricer.PricingResult
     
    Organigram
    Represents a formal organizational structure (organigram).
    Organization
    Represents a formal social and economic entity (Company, NGO, Government).
    PortfolioData.Asset
     
    PortfolioData.Holding
     
    PortfolioOptimizer.Asset
     
    PortfolioOptimizer.EfficientFrontierPoint
     
    PortfolioOptimizer.Portfolio
     
    PotentialResource
    A class representing a potential or virtual resource, such as oil in the soil that is expected to be found, or ingredients required in a recipe or task.
    Process
    Defines various manufacturing and industrial processes.
    Property
    An interface defining ownership and value of assets.
    Resource
    A class representing a physical or virtual resource with ownership, position, and temporal attributes.
    ResourceKind
    An extensible enumeration for resource classifications.
    Task
    Represents a transformation process where materials and human resources are converted into finished products or services.
    TaskProcessor
    An entity capable of performing tasks by consuming resources.
    TaxCalculator.TaxBracket
     
    TaxCalculator.TaxResult
     
    TradeFlowAnalyzer.Country
     
    TradeFlowAnalyzer.TradeFlow
     
    Worker
    Represents a worker within an Organization.
    WorkSituation
    Represents a work situation where individuals interact around a common economic activity, typically at dedicated workplaces.
